content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here your task is to count the number of different paths that achieve the minimum possible path sum.**

Given a binary sequence AAA of length NNN, we construct an N√óNN \times NN√óN matrix BBB as follows:


If i‚â§ji \leq ji‚â§j, then Bi,j=‚àëk=ijAkB_{i,j} = \sum_{k=i}^j A_kBi,j‚Äã=‚àëk=ij‚ÄãAk‚Äã.
Otherwise, Bi,j=Bj,iB_{i,j} = B_{j,i}Bi,j‚Äã=Bj,i‚Äã.
Define a path from (1,1)(1,1)(1,1) to (N,N)(N, N)(N,N) as a sequence of pairs:

P=[(i1,j1),(i2,j2),‚Ä¶,(i2N‚àí1,j2N‚àí1)],P = [(i_1,j_1), (i_2,j_2), \dots, (i_{2N-1},j_{2N-1})],P=[(i1‚Äã,j1‚Äã),(i2‚Äã,j2‚Äã),‚Ä¶,(i2N‚àí1‚Äã,j2N‚àí1‚Äã)],where:


(i1,j1)=(1,1)(i_1, j_1) = (1, 1)(i1‚Äã,j1‚Äã)=(1,1) and (i2N‚àí1,j2N‚àí1)=(N,N)(i_{2N-1}, j_{2N-1}) = (N, N)(i2N‚àí1‚Äã,j2N‚àí1‚Äã)=(N,N).
(ik+1,jk+1)=(ik+1,jk)(i_{k+1}, j_{k+1}) = (i_k + 1, j_k)(ik+1‚Äã,jk+1‚Äã)=(ik‚Äã+1,jk‚Äã) or (ik,jk+1)(i_k, j_k + 1)(ik‚Äã,jk‚Äã+1) for 1‚â§k<2N‚àí11 \leq k < 2N - 11‚â§k<2N‚àí1.
The path sum is defined as ‚àë(i,j)‚ààPBi,j\sum_{(i,j) \in P} B_{i,j}‚àë(i,j)‚ààP‚ÄãBi,j‚Äã.
Your task is to **count the number of different paths** that achieve the **minimum possible path sum**. Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line contains a binary string AAA of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line: **the number of different paths** that achieve the **minimum possible path sum**. Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.
